content:
the Methodist Church in Malaysia
2. (1) The Secretary of the Trustees of the Methodist Church in
Malaysia shall be a body corporate with perpetual succession and
shall have a common seal and in this Act referred to as “the
corporation”.
(2) The corporation is hereby empowered to acquire, purchase,
take, hold and enjoy movable and immovable property of every
description, and to sell, convey, assign, surrender and yield up,
charge, mortgage, demise, re-assign, transfer or otherwise dispose
of, or deal with any movable or immovable property upon such
terms as to the corporation deems fit.
Execution of documents, etc.

Execution of documents, etc.
3. (1) No deed, document or other instrument sealed with the seal
of the corporation shall be deemed to be duly sealed unless such
seal has been affixed in the presence of the person for the time
being holding the office of Secretary of the Trustees of the Methodist
Church in Malaysia, or his attorney duly authorized by a power
of attorney registered under the Powers of Attorney Act 1949 [Act
424], and unless such deed, document or other instrument is signed
by the said person or his attorney.
6
Laws of Malaysia
ACT 457
(2) Such signing shall be taken as sufficient evidence of the due
sealing of such deed, document or other instrument.
Appointment
4.
A notification in the Gazette of the appointment of any person
to hold, or act in, the office of Secretary of the Trustees of the
Methodist Church in Malaysia shall be conclusive evidence that
such person was duly so appointed.
Vesting of property
5.
All movable and immovable property situated in Malaysia
heretofore held, conveyed, assigned, granted, leased, transferred,
transmitted to or otherwise vested in the Secretary of the Methodist
Episcopal Building and Location Board is hereby vested in the
corporation for the respective estates and interests for which the
same is now holden.
6.
(Omitted).
Saving of Crown and other rights
7.
Nothing in this Act shall affect the rights of their Highnesses
the Rulers of the States or of any body politic or corporate or of
any other persons except such as are mentioned in this Act and
those claiming by, from or under them.
Repeal
8.
The Methodist Episcopal Location Board Ordinance of the
Straits Settlements [S.S. Cap. 249], the Methodist Episcopal Location
Board Incorporation Enactment of the Federated Malay States
[F.M.S. Cap. 103], and the Methodist Episcopal Location Board
Incorporation Enactment of the State of Johore [Johore En. No.
145] are hereby repealed.
7
Methodist Church in Malaysia
LAWS OF MALAYSIA
Act 457
